__fixture__.name: restore_rebind_after_reconnect
__fixture__.scenario: A remote connector reconnects after a transient disconnect. The queue enqueues a remote.restore_rebind job to reissue the remote subscription handles, capability envelope, and session lifecycle handles. The job runs on the provider_overlay queue lane under the provider_overlay_budget; it does not consume any hot-path or knowledge-refresh capacity. Surfaces project the provider overlay as warming until the rebind reaches its next phase checkpoint. Cancellation is checkpoint_then_cancel so a second reconnect does not leave split transport state behind.

last_checkpoint.note: Restore-rebind reached its subscription-reissue phase checkpoint; capability envelope reissue is next.

note: Restore-rebind runs exclusively on provider_overlay_budget. No hot-path or knowledge-refresh capacity is borrowed; editor remains ready while surfaces that depend on the remote overlay project warming.
